content.full_text:
I
Re:
Farn Por,rrrcar Pn,rcrrcns C oruurssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(.916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
August 1,2005
Stephen P. Pfahler, City Attomey
Bannan, Green, Frank &Terzian,LLP
555 South Flower Street, Suite 2700
Los Angeles, CA 90071
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-143
Dear Mr. Pfahler:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding South Pasadena City
Councilmember David Margrave on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").r The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person
whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section
8311a@); regulation 18329(a) - copy enclosed.) However, the Commission may also decline to
provide written advice in certain identified circumstances, among which: if the requestor is
seeking advice related to past conduct or if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the
public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(bX8XA & F).)
Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the
requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114;
regulation 18329(b)(7).) Accordingly, after a careful review of the facts you submitted, we
decline to provide written advice to you because it appears that the same or very similar issues
are currently the subject of another pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, and the
request also may involve issues relating to past conduct. Therefore, it would be inappropriate for
the Commission to provide advice on this matter.
I . Government Code sections 8 1000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear al title 2, sections 1 8 I 09 -
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
ffi=


t
Advice File No. W-05-143
Page No. 2
Irrespective of the disposition of the Enforcement case, because the past conduct issues
will remain: we will not be able to provide subsequent advice on this matter.
Our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request.
If you have any questions, please contact the commission at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
willi

Counsel
Legal Division
Enclosure

sections.conclusion: The Commission declines to provide written advice because the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, and the request also may involve issues relating to past conduct. Therefore, it would be inappropriate for the Commission to provide advice on this matter. Because past conduct issues will remain, the Commission will not be able to provide subsequent advice on this matter.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether South Pasadena City Councilmember David Margrave violated conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice on this matter because the issues are the subject of a pending Enforcement Division investigation and may involve past conduct. The Commission states it will not provide subsequent advice on this matter even after disposition of the enforcement case due to the past conduct issues.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with clear explanation of reasons for declining advice. No verbatim QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections labeled as such in document. Question and conclusion synthesized from letter content. Document is clearly structured as FPPC response declining advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether South Pasadena City Councilmember David Margrave violated conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The Commission declines to provide written advice because the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, and the request also may involve issues relating to past conduct. Therefore, it would be inappropriate for the Commission to provide advice on this matter. Because past conduct issues will remain, the Commission will not be able to provide subsequent advice on this matter.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC declination letter declining to provide formal written advice regarding conflict-of-interest questions about South Pasadena City Councilmember David Margrave, citing a pending Enforcement Division investigation and issues relating to past conduct as reasons for the declination.
